snippet: Schools must upload their student achievement data (teacher judgements) for each curriculum area taught for every student enrolled at the school. This is uploaded to the department twice yearly via CASES21. The first upload occurs at the end of semester 1 and must be completed by June 30. The second upload occurs at the end of semester 2, prior to December 31 and prior to the school running the CASES21 Admin end of year rollover task. To enable the department to process the data, schools must manage their student achievement data in two mandatory steps: Video guides showing how to complete these steps are available below. Schools can view the data they have uploaded into CASES21 via the School Information Portal. This achievement data from CASES21 is included in the Annual Report to the school community. Student reporting software packages provided by commercial suppliers enable schools to report on student achievement against the Victorian Curriculum F–10 achievement standards, as reflected in the school's teaching and learning plan.
